text: Central Limit Theorem: sum of independent random variables approaches normal distribution — reason why normal distribution appears everywhere

Probability theory quantifies uncertainty — fundamental to machine learning, statistics, cryptography, and randomized algorithms. Key concepts: random variables, probability distributions (Bernoulli, binomial, normal, Poisson), expectation, variance, Bayes' theorem, law of large numbers.
Used in: Naive Bayes classifiers, Bayesian inference, spam filters.
Central Limit Theorem: sum of independent random variables approaches normal distribution — reason why normal distribution appears everywhere.
